description: Disposition inventory for legacy ADDIN DOCSTYLE field-code payload keys (writer v3, readers 1-3; see inst/schema/docstyle-field-codes.json and the docstyle_schemas required/optional lists in R/field_codes.R). lib.migrate consumes this file to decide, for every key on a legacy payload, whether it is written into the v4 field envelope (mapped), preserved in the migration record body (record), or discarded with a documented reason (dropped). Any legacy key not listed here is an unmapped-legacy-key error finding, by design -- this file must stay in lockstep with the legacy schema.
policyDefaultRule: Legacy field codes carry no explicit policy field, so migrate.lua derives field-envelope.v4's required 'policy' purely from payload type (see payloadTypes[type].policy above, applied by migrate.payload as a default after the per-key walk). section, float and anchor payloads are layout/structural regions the renderer regenerates each time (policy=structural); char, div, list, table and figure payloads carry author-authored content the tool must preserve verbatim across regenerations (policy=authored-preserve). 'float' is a legacy alias of 'anchor' (see R/field_codes.R: dispatch table comment 'backward compat', and test-field-codes.R 'float type dispatches to anchor handler (backward compat)'), which is why both share kind=anchor and policy=structural.
idResolutionRule: field-envelope.v4's 'id' is populated by whichever key in this file is disposition=mapped with target='id' (currently 'name', used by div payloads, and 'id', used by figure payloads -- both are the legacy payload's own identity key, just named differently per type). When no such key is present on the legacy payload, migrate.payload assigns a deterministic placeholder 'g-<kind>-migr01' (kind = the payload's translated envelope kind, not the raw legacy type; ordinal is always the fixed literal '01' because migrate.payload is a single-input/single-output pure function with no cross-call counter to thread -- see the comment in lib/migrate.lua). The 'g-' prefix intentionally mirrors lib/ids.lua's reserved generated-id prefix (ids.lua's check_explicit() rejects author-supplied ids starting with 'g-') so a placeholder id is recognizable as tool-generated, not authored, without migrate.lua depending on ids.lua's random generation (which would make migration non-reproducible).

type.note: Handled by a dedicated pre-check in migrate.payload (payloadTypes lookup; unknown type -> unknown-payload-type error, envelope nil), not by the generic per-key writer -- envelope.kind is the translated payloadTypes[type].kind, never a verbatim copy of the raw legacy 'type' string. Listed here only so every observed legacy key has a recorded disposition (completeness check).

version.note: Handled by a dedicated pre-check in migrate.payload: missing version defaults to 1 (mirrors R/field_codes.R:520 `payload$version %||% 1L`), then version < 1 or > 3 is rejected with unsupported-version (envelope nil). The envelope's 'v' is always the literal constant 4 (field-envelope.v4 requires v=4 for every migrated record; 'v' and legacy 'version' are conceptually analogous fields, not the same value) -- target='v' documents that correspondence, it is not a verbatim value copy. Listed here only for key-inventory completeness.

name.note: Author-assigned identity for div payloads (e.g. name='abstract', name='version-history'). Generic walk: when present, becomes envelope.id verbatim. See idResolutionRule.

id.note: Author-assigned QMD figure id (e.g. id='fig-consort-flow'). Figures use 'id' instead of 'name' for the same identity role; generic walk treats it the same way. See idResolutionRule.

class.note: CSS class hook selecting the payload's style/positioning rules (e.g. 'date', 'list-alpha', 'section-body', 'table-formal', 'column-margin'). Not an envelope property; needed to reconstruct rendering, so it lands in the migration record body.

source.note: Literal QMD shortcode source the char span expands from (e.g. '[{{< meta version-summary.date >}}]{.date}'). RENAMED IN THE RECORD: migrate.payload stores this value under 'legacySource', not 'source', because hashes.content_hash() strips any key literally named 'hash' or 'source' at every depth (lib/hashes.lua STRIP table). That strip convention exists for *provenance pointers* (e.g. {file, start} in test-hashes.lua), but here 'source' is semantic content, not provenance -- keeping the legacy key name would silently exclude the shortcode text from the envelope hash, so an edit to the char's source text would never change its content hash. The rename keeps the value inside the hash without touching lib/hashes.lua (out of scope for this task). This is the only record key whose name differs from its legacy key.

start.note: Starting number for an ordered list continuation.

page-break.note: Whether the section opens on a new page.

line-numbers.note: Line-numbering mode for the section ('continuous', 'restart-page', etc.).

widths.note: Comma-separated column width percentages (e.g. '30,70'). Explicitly required to land in the migration record, not the envelope (task brief Step 2).

width.note: Overall table width (table) or image width (figure) as a CSS length/percentage. Same key name, related-but-distinct meaning per type; disposition is identical either way.

header-bold.note: Whether the table header row is bold.

docpr_id.note: Word wp:docPr/@id integer; stable OOXML identity anchor for the figure's drawing element.

original_path.note: Source image path at render time, restored on re-harvest (#79).

alt.note: Accessibility alt text for the figure image.

vertical_anchor.note: Vertical anchor reference: text | margin | page | section.

horizontal_anchor.note: Horizontal anchor reference: text | margin | page.

position_y.note: Vertical offset from anchor (CSS length or dxa).

position_x.note: Horizontal offset from anchor (CSS length or dxa).

float_width.note: Width of the anchored element (CSS length or dxa).

wrap_style.note: Text wrap type: none | square | tight | top-and-bottom.

wrap_side.note: Text wrap side: both | left | right | largest.

wrap_distance.note: Wrap spacing, 1-4 value CSS shorthand (top right bottom left).

adjacent.rationale: Anchor-target-adjacency is documented as deferred and unimplemented upstream (inst/schema/docstyle-field-codes.json anchor_payload_fields.adjacent: 'Anchor target element ID (deferred, #117)'). No consumer of this value exists yet, so migration discards it with an info finding rather than carrying dead data forward into the v4 record. Revisit -- change disposition to record or mapped -- once #117 lands and something actually reads it.

content_hint.note: Advisory content type for the anchored region: table | image | text | mixed.

z_layer.note: Stacking layer: front | behind (maps to behindDoc on wp:anchor).

content_mode.note: Assembly mechanism for the anchored content: auto | textbox.

caption_y.note: Vertical offset of the caption within the anchor's group coordinate space (DXA).

image_height.note: Height of the image member within the anchor's group (DXA).
